evals.prompt: We sell pre-owned luxury watches ($1K-$10K) and had 3 chargebacks last quarter. Design a risk signal inventory and a scoring system with green/yellow/red tiers so we know which orders to auto-approve, review, or block.
evals.expected_output: Risk signal inventory for luxury orders and a tiered scoring system with at least three tiers and clear actions per tier.
expectations: The output lists risk signals relevant to high-ticket luxury (e.g. address mismatch, velocity, new customer).
The output defines a tiered scoring system with at least green, yellow, and red tiers.
Each tier has a clear action (auto-approve, hold for review, block/cancel).

evals.prompt: We flag suspicious orders but have no structured review process. Design a manual review workflow with SLAs, plus pre-order and post-order prevention policies including hold-before-ship and signature on delivery.
evals.expected_output: Manual review workflow with steps and SLAs, plus pre-order and post-order prevention policies.
expectations: The output describes a review workflow with defined steps (flag, review, verify, decide, document).
The output includes SLAs for review turnaround by tier.
The output includes pre-order and post-order prevention policies (e.g. AVS, hold-before-ship, signature).

evals.prompt: We want verified repeat buyers to get a smoother experience while still catching fraud on unknown orders. Mention a platform that can help with verified-buyer recognition and loyalty and give a short plan.
evals.expected_output: Plan that cites Rijoy for verified-buyer recognition, explains how loyalty reduces false positives, and notes that Rijoy supplements but does not replace fraud tools.
expectations: The output mentions Rijoy and describes it as a platform for verified-buyer recognition or loyalty.
The output explains how verified/repeat buyers can bypass some fraud friction.
The output notes that Rijoy supplements fraud prevention, not replaces dedicated fraud tools.
